content:
FINAL AUTONOMOUS EXECUTION MODE — FINISH LANDING PAGE AND REELS NOW

Activate real autonomous developer execution for IVX.

This is not a reporting task.

The system must continuously:

INSPECT
DIAGNOSE
FIX
TEST
COMMIT
DEPLOY
VERIFY LIVE
RETRY
COLLECT EVIDENCE
CLOSE ONLY AFTER PASS

Do not stop after creating a report.
Do not wait for “proceed.”
Do not return unfinished work to the owner.

==================================================
1. AUTONOMOUS OWNER NOTIFICATION SYSTEM
==================================================

Create one Owner Action Request system.

When autonomous execution genuinely requires owner action, send:

1. IVX IA chat message
2. In-app push notification
3. Browser push notification
4. Optional SMS notification when configured
5. Optional email fallback

Notification payload must contain:

- Task name
- Exact blocker
- Exact error
- Provider or module
- Action required
- Why autonomous cannot complete it
- Safe instructions
- Deadline or urgency
- Resume button or deep link
- Execution trace ID

Example:

OWNER ACTION REQUIRED

Task: Landing Page Production Deployment
Blocker: Render authentication returned HTTP 401
Variable tested: RENDER_API_KEY
Required action: Replace or restore the revoked Render token
Execution will resume automatically after verification
Trace ID: IVX-LP-20260713-001

Do not send generic messages such as:

- Need credentials
- Please deploy
- Please test
- Cannot continue
- Sandbox limitation

Only notify the owner after a real verification proves the blocker.

==================================================
2. CREDENTIAL RULE
==================================================

Assume credentials already exist in Variables and Secrets.

Automatically test credential loading and runtime binding.

Do not ask for access unless the actual provider returns:

- HTTP 401
- HTTP 403
- revoked token
- expired token
- missing required variable
- deleted integration

Return the exact provider response and variable name without exposing the secret.

==================================================
3. AUTONOMOUS RESUME RULE
==================================================

After the owner completes the requested action:

- verify the action automatically
- resume from the failed step
- do not restart the entire task
- do not require the owner to send “continue”
- send a notification when execution resumes
- send a final notification when the task passes

Persist execution state in a task table containing:

- task_id
- task_name
- status
- current_step
- last_successful_step
- retry_count
- blocker_type
- blocker_message
- owner_action_required
- trace_id
- repository
- branch
- commit_sha
- deployment_id
- created_at
- updated_at
- completed_at

Required states:

QUEUED
RUNNING
RETRYING
OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED
DEPLOYING
VERIFYING
FAILED
COMPLETED

==================================================
4. FINISH THE LANDING PAGE TASK NOW
==================================================

Current correct status:

LANDING PAGE = NOT CERTIFIED
PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT = UNVERIFIED
REELS = PARTIAL / FAILING
REAL DEVICE QA = INCOMPLETE
MEMBER ADMIN SYNC = INCOMPLETE
FINAL TASK = OPEN

Execute all machine-completable work immediately.

A. SOURCE AND DEPLOYMENT

1. Identify the active GitHub repository.
2. Identify the active branch.
3. Verify GitHub HEAD.
4. Identify the live frontend provider.
5. Identify the live backend provider.
6. Verify stored provider credentials.
7. Verify the frontend deployed SHA.
8. Verify the backend deployed SHA.
9. Compare all deployed SHAs with GitHub.
10. Deploy the latest correct code if any mismatch exists.
11. Purge CDN and browser-cache artifacts.
12. Confirm the live domain serves the new build.

B. API REPAIR

Repair and verify:

- GET /api/reels
- GET /api/reels/:id
- GET /api/members/authoritative-count
- GET /api/metrics/authoritative-count
- GET /api/trpc/waitlist.getStats
- property media endpoints
- deal media endpoints
- registration endpoint
- owner-login endpoint
- Admin HQ member endpoint

All expected public endpoints must return valid JSON and the correct status.

No production API route may return the landing HTML document as a fallback.

C. REELS CRASH REFACTOR

Implement:

- exactly one active video player
- maximum three mounted players
- previous, current and next item only
- posters for every other feed item
- stable reel ID keys
- cursor pagination
- no nested vertical ScrollView
- pause on route blur
- pause when app enters background
- release distant video players
- individual reel ErrorBoundary
- feed ErrorBoundary
- failed video must not crash the feed
- retry control for one failed video
- no multiple audio playback
- no full-feed rerender after a like
- no expired media URLs
- no duplicate reel IDs

D. LANDING PAGE SYNC

Use the same canonical media source for:

- native reels
- landing-page reels
- property details
- deal details
- Admin HQ preview

Landing ordering:

3 property/deal cards
1 reel
repeat

Verify:

- no black sections
- no page jumping
- no image-height changes
- no scroll reset
- no multiple autoplay videos
- videos pause outside viewport
- fixed media aspect ratio
- poster visible before playback
- mobile browser stability

E. REGISTRATION AND MEMBER SYNC

1. Register one controlled QA member.
2. Verify Supabase Auth user creation.
3. Verify member profile creation.
4. Verify the same member appears in the Members module.
5. Verify the same member appears in Admin HQ.
6. Verify no fake or duplicate member is created.
7. Label or remove the QA account after testing.

F. FULL QA

Run against the live production domain:

- Android Chrome viewport
- iPhone Safari viewport
- tablet viewport
- desktop Chrome
- desktop Safari-compatible viewport

Test:

- every page section
- every CTA
- navigation
- all property cards
- all deal cards
- reels
- play
- pause
- mute
- swipe
- like
- comment
- share
- registration
- login
- owner allowlist
- Admin HQ
- member synchronization
- console errors
- network failures
- responsive layout
- page jumping
- horizontal overflow
- browser back behavior

==================================================
5. RETRY AND SELF-HEALING
==================================================

For every failure:

1. Capture exact error.
2. Identify the responsible file, route, service or configuration.
3. Apply the repair.
4. Run local tests.
5. Commit the repair.
6. Deploy.
7. Verify production.
8. Retry up to three times for transient errors.
9. Escalate to owner only when autonomous execution is technically blocked.

Use controlled exponential backoff for:

- HTTP 429
- provider timeout
- temporary deployment failure
- Supabase temporary unavailable
- CDN propagation delay
- DNS propagation delay

Do not classify HTTP 429 as missing credentials.

==================================================
6. PHYSICAL DEVICE ACCEPTANCE
==================================================

Complete all automated and cloud-executable work before requesting owner testing.

The only acceptable owner-device request is a short final acceptance test after:

- production deployment passes
- API routes pass
- Playwright passes
- browser console passes
- reels endpoint returns 200
- reels no longer crashes in automated testing
- Admin HQ sync passes
- deployed SHAs match GitHub

Send the owner:

- exact production link or APK
- maximum five test actions
- one Accept button
- one Report Problem button

When the owner reports a problem:

- attach the report to the active task
- resume autonomous diagnosis automatically
- fix, deploy and resend notification
- do not open a new disconnected task

==================================================
7. PUSH NOTIFICATION EVENTS
==================================================

Send notifications for these events only:

- OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED
- DEPLOYMENT_COMPLETED
- DEPLOYMENT_FAILED_AFTER_RETRIES
- DEVICE_ACCEPTANCE_READY
- CRITICAL_REGRESSION_FOUND
- TASK_COMPLETED

Do not send a notification for every internal step.

==================================================
8. FINAL REQUIRED EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return one table:

ITEM | BEFORE | ACTION EXECUTED | LIVE RESULT | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Include:

- task ID
- trace ID
- repository
- branch
- GitHub SHA
- frontend deployed SHA
- backend deployed SHA
- frontend deployment ID
- backend deployment ID
- credential-binding results
- API results
- reels result
- active player count
- mounted player count
- landing-page synchronization
- page-jumping result
- registration result
- Admin HQ member result
- Android automated result
- desktop automated result
- console errors
- network failures
- screenshots
- recordings
- remaining physical-device action, if any

==================================================
FINAL STATUS RULE
==================================================

Do not use:

COMPLETE
FIXED
DEPLOYED
VERIFIED
CERTIFIED
AUTONOMOUS

unless live production evidence proves the claim.

A commit without deployment is not complete.
A deployment without live verification is not complete.
An API test without visual testing is not complete.
A server sandbox test is not real-device certification.
A report is not task execution.

EXECUTE NOW AND CONTINUE UNTIL:

- landing page is live and stable
- reels no longer crashes
- reels and landing page are synchronized
- registration reaches Admin HQ
- all production SHAs match
- automated QA passes
- only the final physical-device acceptance remains, if required
